title: Solenoid Valve Calibration for Behavior Rigs Utilizing Water Reward
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2ggga","text":"Solenoid valve calibration is an important maintenance step to perform on behavior rigs that use water as a reward to ensure that reward volumes remain consistent over time. Calibration is the process by which the linear relationship between valve open time and drop size is defined for each lickspout. This process produces a linear best-fit curve of two points by recording drop sizes at set valve open times. Spot checks serve to verify that the open time calculated by the calibration curve results in a drop size that is within the acceptable range of the desired drop size (+/- 15%). The Dynamic Foraging GUI uses the calibration curve to estimate the necessary valve open time to deliver the desired water drop size (reward) during experiments. This protocol outlines the parameters, steps, and frequency of water calibration and spot checks.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":594,"length":20}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"76BF844B432A447A9791900CA3A823BD","url":"https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/dynamic-foraging-task"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ar9c","text":"This protocol is used for behavior rigs with a water system. Software can be used (such as the Dynamic Foraging GUI) to easily calculate values that are out of range and track historic calibrations, but all calculations can be done by hand. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":95,"length":20}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"870AE5CEB7554AD182E62D7D39C2FAB6","url":"https://github.com/AllenNeuralDynamics/dynamic-foraging-task"}}}}

steps: Clear each water line by free-running at least 10 mL of water through them. For setups with one lickspout, clear the single water line. If using the Dynamic Foraging GUI, this can be done easily through the water calibration window (Open left/right 5 mL ). This step ensures that all air bubbles are cleared from the lines. Figure 1: Water calibration window in the Dynamic Foraging GUI
Refill the water reservoirs to 50 mL .
Zero a scale of 0.001 g precision.

Retrieve a weigh boat, weigh it on the scale, and note the weight. If using the dynamic foraging GUI, all weights can be noted in their corresponding text boxes, for example: Calibrate left, weight before (g) (see Figure 2). The GUI will automatically do all calculations for you. Figure 2: Multi-valve calibration section of the Dynamic Foraging GUI
Place the weigh boat beneath the lickspouts.

Start the calibration (for rigs with two lickspouts, start the left side calibration) with the first calibration point (1000 drops at 20ms valve open time). If using the Dynamic Foraging GUI, confirm that valve open time is 20s in the dropdown menu for open time (s). See Figure 2.
Remove the weigh boat once 1000 drops have been delivered. Ensure to gently swipe any remaining water on the lickspout tip into the weigh boat.
Weigh the weigh boat on the scale and record the weight. If using the Dynamic Foraging GUI, record weight in the multi-valve calibration weight after (g) popup. See Figure 2.

Empty the weigh boat. If using the Dynamic Foraging GUI, select Continue. See Figure 2. If any mistakes are made during measurements, the user can select Repeat to repeat the previous calibration point.

Proceed with the second calibration point (1000 drops at 30ms valve open time). If using the Dynamic Foraging GUI, confirm that valve open time is 30s in the dropdown menu for open time (s). See Figure 2.
Repeat go to step #7 .
Refill the water reservoir to the 50 mL mark.
For rigs with a single lickspout, calibration is complete.

Clear each water line by free-running at least 10 mL of water through them. If using the Dynamic Foraging GUI, this can be done easily through the water calibration window (Open left/right 5 mL ). This step ensures that all air bubbles are cleared from the lines.
Refill the water reservoirs to the 50 mL mark.
Zero a scale of 0.001 g precision.

Retrieve a weigh boat, weigh it on the scale, and note the weight. If using the dynamic foraging GUI, all weights can be noted in their corresponding text boxes, for example: Check left, weight before (g) (see Figure 4). The GUI will automatically do all calculations for you. Figure 4: Spot check section of the Dynamic Foraging GUI
Start the spot check (for rigs with two lickspouts, run the left side spot check), with 200 drops at 20ms valve open time.
Remove the weigh boat once 200 drops have been delivered. Ensure to gently swipe any remaining water on the lickspout tip into the weigh boat.
Weigh the weigh boat on the scale and record the weight. If using the Dynamic Foraging GUI, record weight in the check left weight after (g) popup. See Figure 4.
Empty the weigh boat.
For rigs with two lickspouts, run a spot check on the right side ( go to step #22 ) . For rigs with a single lickspout, spot check is complete, go to step #29 .

Make note of any spot check reports that are outside of the acceptable range during this process. Full water calibration (steps 1 - 17) calculates a linear expression (see Figure 1) with the lower and upper acceptable bounds of -15% and +15%. If a spot check falls outside of this range, a full calibration must be performed for that valve. See calculations below: Average volume per drop = ((final tube weight - empty tube weight (g))/number of water drops * 1000) Difference = average volume per water drop (g) - supposed volume** Tolerance = average volume per drop (g) * 0.15 If difference > tolerance, spot check fails **specified by GUI, 0.002mL

If a spot check fails, go to step #1 and run through the entire calibration regimen again for that lickspout and repeat the spot check. If all spot checks pass, go to step #30 . If a spot check fails immediately following a re-calibration, or if > 2 spot checks fail within 30 days, the solenoid must be replaced and the box in question should not be used until it can pass a spot check again.
Empty the weigh boat.
Refill the water reservoir to the 50 mL mark.
Close the GUI.
Place the weigh boat beneath the lickspouts.
Place the weigh boat beneath the lickspouts.
For rigs with two lickspouts, go to step #4 for the right side.
Weigh the empty weigh boat and note the new starting weight. If using the Dynamic Foraging GUI, enter the new starting weight in the multi-valve calibration weight before (g) popup. See Figure 2.
Weigh the empty weigh boat and note the new starting weight. If using the Dynamic Foraging GUI, enter the new starting weight in the spot check weight before (g) popup. See Figure 4.

Calibration is complete, press Finished. Calibration should yield a curve similar to the linear expression below. Proceed to spot check. The calibration curve is computed by performing a linear regression where X is open time and Y is average volume of one drop. Figure 3: Calibration curve in the Dynamic Foraging GUI (includes spot checks)
creator: Ella Hilton-VanOsdall
